behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff (request builder -> API client). A pure unit test of a helper could miss dropped fields during wiring. Rejected System because behavior text is about request contents, not real network/OS effects; a stub transport can expose omission. Rejected Agentic because correctness does not depend on model judgment/output.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a stub API client that records outbound payloads. Invoke classify() with a sample document and taxonomy, then assert recorded request contains all valid labels exactly once in the expected field.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is at response-handling boundary: API adapter output must be validated before returning to caller. A unit test of normalization alone may miss bypasses in orchestration. Rejected Agentic because this behavior is about handling an invalid returned token, not model quality; stubbed response is sufficient. Rejected System because no real network/OS behavior is required.
behaviors.test_description: Use module with a stub API client that returns an out-of-taxonomy label (e.g., "medical"). Call classify(); assert returned value is "unknown" and raw label is not leaked.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets one local normalization step with identical input -> identical output; no module interaction or I/O needed. Rejected Integration because behavior text scopes to deterministic normalization logic itself.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function/object repeatedly with the same raw response fixture and taxonomy; assert exact same normalized label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI API calls and consistency of model-produced labels; stubs would hide real model/provider variation. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-agent/multi-step pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N live classifications for the same document+taxonomy against the real provider; record provider raw responses and final labels. Assert final labels are stable whenever raw provider response is unchanged, and only differ when provider response differs.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when token-budget enforcement and dispatch wiring interact (pre-dispatch gate). Unit test of tokenizer helper alone can miss accidental send despite over-budget. Rejected System because real network/subprocess is not required to observe whether dispatch was blocked/modified. Rejected Agentic because no dependence on model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget and stub API client. Submit an over-budget document. Assert module applies configured policy (truncate or reject) and that outbound request/token estimate respects budget before any dispatch.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Requires real provider/model call because provider token accounting/tokenization can differ from local estimates; mocks can falsely pass and hide cost overruns. Although assertion is deterministic, behavior passes through real AI API and depends on provider-produced usage metadata. Rejected Workflow because only one model step is evaluated.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification on representative documents with the real provider. Capture provider-reported token usage from responses and assert each call is <= configured per-call budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is retry orchestration between API client wrapper, backoff policy, and caller-facing return path. Stubbed rate-limit responses plus controllable clock can reproduce it. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network timing quirks, only correct handling of provider signal. Rejected Agentic because no dependence on model decision quality/content.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub API client that returns rate-limit error then success. Invoke classify() with fake/controllable clock, assert module waits/backs off, retries expected count, and eventually returns successful label instead of surfacing rate-limit error immediately.
